anzsic_codes.name: Fixed Space Heating, Cooling and Ventilation Equipment Manufacturing
Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
On 27 February 2026, Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (an affiliate of various funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc. (**Blackstone**)) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to acquire all the issued shares in Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (**ACT**).

ACT is a US-based engineering and manufacturing company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling solutions for a range of industries. ACT provides passive and active heat transfer technologies, including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops, thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial markets.

Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (Rocket) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in
Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (ACT), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Blackstone, Inc. (Blackstone) is a US-based investment management
firm. Rocket is a special purpose vehicle that is an affiliate of various
funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone.
The target, ACT, is a US-based engineering and manufacturing
company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling
solutions. It supplies passive and active heat transfer technologies,
including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops,
thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for
applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data
centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial
markets.
The application indicates that no funds advised and/or managed by
affiliates of Blackstone have an interest in any business that
competes in relation to any of the products supplied by ACT in
Australia, or acquires products from, or supplies products or services
to, ACT in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a) There is no horizontal overlap in Australia between the
products and services supplied by the parties, and there are
no vertical relationships.
b) ACT has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
